title: senecio poeppigii
intro: Senecio poeppigii, also known as Andean wormwood, is a plant species native to the Andes region in South America, characterized by its grey-green foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, treat fever, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues such as diarrhea and nausea. The health benefits of Senecio poeppigii include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve circulation, and provide antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, Senecio poeppigii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and folk medicine.
